branches.name: main
branches.description: The foundational text of the Oral Torah, compiled by Rabbi Yehuda HaNasi around 200 CE. This is the root from which all branches derive.

branches.name: bavli
branches.description: The more widely studied of the two Talmuds, compiled in the Babylonian academies (primarily Sura and Pumbedita) and redacted around 500–600 CE. The halakha generally follows the Bavli.

manuscript.name: Munich Manuscript (Cod. Hebr. 95)
manuscript.note: The only complete manuscript of the Babylonian Talmud

branches.name: yerushalmi
branches.description: The earlier Talmud, compiled in the academies of the Land of Israel (primarily Tiberias and Caesarea) around 350–400 CE. Shorter and often considered less edited than the Bavli.
branches.origin_location: Tiberias and Caesarea, Land of Israel

manuscript.name: Leiden Manuscript (Cod. Or. 4720)
manuscript.note: The only complete manuscript of the Jerusalem Talmud

branches.name: rashi-commentary
branches.description: Rashi's commentary (11th century, France) is not a separate version of the text but an annotation layer applied primarily to the Bavli. Represented here as a branch to visualize its contributions.

branches.name: tosafot-commentary
branches.description: Critical glosses by the Tosafists (12th–13th century, France and Germany), often responding directly to Rashi's commentary.
